description_long: This is a tall, double-gourd shaped porcelain vase with a slender neck and a bulbous lower body. The surface is predominantly white, adorned with intricate cobalt blue floral motifs and scrolling vine patterns, reminiscent of traditional Chinese blue-and-white ceramics. The upper and lower bulbs are decorated with large, detailed lotus blossoms, while the neck features elongated leaf designs. The glaze is glossy, giving the vase a lustrous, reflective finish. The overall craftsmanship suggests a decorative purpose, suitable for display in refined urban environments. The vase appears delicate, with no visible handles or additional structural features.
description: A glossy, double-gourd porcelain vase with blue floral and vine motifs on a white background, featuring a slender neck and bulbous body.
description_view_0: Front view showing the prominent blue lotus motif and scrolling vines on the lower bulb, with the neck's leaf pattern clearly visible.
description_view_1: This view highlights a slightly different arrangement of the floral motifs, with the lotus blossom and vines wrapping around the body.
description_view_2: The side view reveals the continuity of the blue floral and vine patterns, emphasizing the vase's rounded form and slender neck.
description_view_3: This angle shows more of the vine scrollwork and less of the central lotus motif, focusing on the curvature of the lower bulb.
